system.name: Retirement Planning SaaS Platform
system.description: Multi-user commercial SaaS for retirement cost comparison, financial projections, and Monte Carlo portfolio simulation. Uses Claude AI agents for automated cost data research, location data updates, and data refinement. Dashboard provides personalized retirement planning with tax calculations, Social Security modeling, and RMD projections.
system.deploymentEnvironment: Docker Compose production stack — self-hosted with Tailscale TLS (nginx reverse proxy + Fastify API + PostgreSQL + Redis). Public access via Tailscale Funnel with auto TLS termination.

aiUsage.agentOrchestration: Claude Code agents automate cost data research, location data updates, and data refinement via packages/tools/
aiUsage.clientSideAI: None — all financial calculations (tax, SS, RMD, Monte Carlo) are deterministic pure functions, no AI in the calculation path
aiUsage.dataCollection: AI agents research and update location cost data from public sources; users do not interact with AI directly through the dashboard
aiUsage.decisionMaking: No automated decisions about users — the platform presents financial projections for users to make their own retirement planning decisions

riskClassification.notes: The platform uses AI agents for backend data research only — users do not interact with AI directly. Financial projections are deterministic pure functions. However, the platform operates in the financial services sector and presents projections that consumers may rely on for retirement decisions, triggering high-risk classification in Colorado (consequential decisions) and Brazil (financial sector). EU classification is limited-risk because no automated decisions are made about users — the system presents information for users to make their own decisions. Content labeling applies because AI-researched cost data should be disclosed.

personalDataTypes.source: User input during onboarding and settings

personalDataTypes.fields: ssPia, ssFra, ssClaimAge per household member
personalDataTypes.source: User input in SS benefits tab

personalDataTypes.fields: adultsCount, member names, birth years, roles
pet type, breed, size, birth year, expected lifespan
UI state, selected locations, projection parameters (~48 keys)
personalDataTypes.source: User interactions stored as JSONB blob
User input in Manage Locations tab

personalDataTypes.fields: What-if retirement simulation parameters and results

dataSubjects: Household dependents (spouses, referenced but not registered)
Admin users (SaaS staff managing location data)

legalBasis.identity: Contract — required to provide the retirement planning service
legalBasis.financial: Contract — necessary to perform portfolio projections and retirement calculations
legalBasis.socialSecurity: Consent — optional data provided to enable SS benefit modeling
legalBasis.household: Consent — optional data to enable household-level projections
legalBasis.preferences: Legitimate interest — service improvement and UX continuity
legalBasis.subscription: Contract — payment processing and feature gating
legalBasis.technical: Legitimate interest — security monitoring and fraud detection

q1_purpose_limitation.notes: Data collected only for retirement planning projections and account management

q2_data_minimization.notes: Only financial data needed for projections is collected; no SSNs, no bank account numbers, no investment account details

q3_storage_limitation.notes: No retention policy implemented yet — data persists indefinitely until user deletion

q4_accuracy.notes: Users can update all personal and financial data at any time via dashboard

q5_security.notes: AES-256-GCM field-level encryption implemented for financial data. Clerk JWT auth on all protected routes. Per-tier rate limiting (free=60, basic=120, premium=300, admin=600 req/min) with Redis backing. TLS via Tailscale certs (auto-detected by nginx entrypoint). HSTS header enforced. nginx reverse proxy keeps API internal (not publicly exposed).

risks.description: Financial data (portfolio balance, SS benefits) accessed by unauthorized parties
risks.mitigation: Clerk JWT auth middleware on all protected routes (implemented), AES-256-GCM field-level encryption (implemented), per-tier rate limiting with Redis (implemented), nginx reverse proxy — API not publicly exposed (implemented)

risks.description: Database compromise exposing financial and identity data
risks.mitigation: AES-256-GCM encryption at rest for financial fields (implemented), Prisma parameterized queries (implemented), nginx security headers — CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Permissions-Policy, HSTS (implemented), TLS in transit via Tailscale certs (implemented)

risks.description: EU user data stored on US servers without adequate safeguards
risks.mitigation: Standard Contractual Clauses (TODO), EU-US DPF certification (TODO), DPA with cloud provider (TODO)

risks.description: User A accessing User B's financial data via API
risks.mitigation: Prisma where: { userId } on all queries (TODO — routes currently return 501)

risks.description: Auth provider or payment processor mishandling user data
User data not fully cascaded on account deletion
risks.mitigation: Cascade delete defined in Prisma schema (implemented), but endpoint not tested

risks.description: No audit trail of when users consented to data processing
Portfolio balance or SS PIA values appearing in server logs or error messages
risks.mitigation: Log sanitization (TODO), Fastify logger serializer customization (TODO)

risks.description: Sensitive financial data persisted in browser localStorage
risks.mitigation: Zustand stores hold portfolio in memory only; localStorage stores only UI preferences (implemented)

risks.description: Claude agents produce inaccurate location cost data that misleads users
risks.mitigation: Human review of AI-generated data before publishing (implemented), financial disclaimer (TODO)

aiRisks.description: AI agents may source cost data disproportionately from English-language sources, underrepresenting local pricing in Latin America
aiRisks.mitigation: Cross-reference with local government sources and expat community data

aiRisks.description: AI agent fabricates or misattributes cost data for locations
aiRisks.mitigation: All AI output reviewed by human before database insertion; validate-location-data skill checks for anomalies

aiRisks.description: Users cannot verify whether location cost data was AI-researched or human-verified
Users treat AI-enhanced retirement projections as financial advice
aiRisks.mitigation: Financial disclaimer prominently displayed (TODO): 'Not financial advice — consult a professional'

aiRisks.description: System depends on Anthropic Claude API availability for data updates
aiRisks.mitigation: AI agents are only used for data maintenance, not runtime; dashboard functions without AI

aiRisks.description: Cost projections may systematically disadvantage certain demographics or locations
aiRisks.mitigation: Bias testing across location categories, income levels, and household compositions (TODO)

rectification.notes: Users can update all personal data via dashboard PUT endpoints

erasure.notes: DELETE /api/me endpoint exists as stub (501); Prisma cascade deletes defined in schema

restriction.notes: No mechanism to restrict processing without deletion

objection.notes: No mechanism for users to object to specific processing activities

automatedDecisions.notes: No automated decisions made about users — all calculations are presented as information, user makes own decisions

withdrawConsent.notes: No consent withdrawal mechanism beyond account deletion

lodgeComplaint.notes: No link to relevant data protection authority

information.notes: No privacy notice or data processing disclosure published

implemented: TLS/HTTPS via Tailscale certs with auto-detect nginx entrypoint
HSTS header (max-age=31536000, includeSubDomains)
nginx reverse proxy — API container not publicly exposed, internal Docker network only
AES-256-GCM field-level encryption for financial data (portfolio, income, SS PIAs)
Clerk JWT auth middleware on all protected API routes
Admin role enforcement on /api/admin/* routes
Server-side tier enforcement via requireTier() middleware — free users blocked from premium API endpoints
Zod input validation on all request bodies (including billing/checkout and health/cleanup)
Prototype pollution defense — safeJsonRecord sanitizer strips __proto__/constructor/prototype from JSONB inputs
Per-tier rate limiting (free=60, basic=120, premium=300, admin=600 req/min) with Redis backing
Prisma parameterized queries (no SQL injection)
React textContent rendering (no XSS)
Delegated auth to Clerk (no password storage)
nginx security headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options SAMEORIGIN, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, X-XSS-Protection, Permissions-Policy (camera/mic/geo denied)
Content Security Policy with Clerk and Stripe domains whitelisted
server_tokens off (nginx version hidden)
Sentry error monitoring (opt-in via DSN env var)
GDPR: right to erasure (DELETE /api/me), right to portability (GET /api/me/export with decrypted data)
Idempotent webhook processing (processed_events table + cleanup scheduler)
HTTP→HTTPS redirect on port 8080 when TLS certs present
Tailscale Funnel for public access with automatic TLS termination
Same-origin API routing eliminates CORS attack surface
Gzip compression for text/css/json/js/xml/wasm
Health endpoint hides config details (encryption/auth/stripe status, memory) from unauthenticated requests
Generic error messages in production — no stack traces or DB schema leakage
Encryption key validation on startup — warns loudly in production if ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY missing
planned: WAF (Cloudflare or similar) for public deployment

dpoConsultation.notes: No DPO designated — required if processing large-scale financial data of EU residents. Recommend designating a DPO or outsourcing to a DPO-as-a-service provider before launch.

outcome.summary: The platform collects sensitive financial data (portfolio balances, SS benefits) from consumers for retirement planning. Auth middleware (Clerk JWT) and field-level encryption (AES-256-GCM) are now fully implemented. Per-tier rate limiting with Redis backing is in production. TLS is enforced via Tailscale certs with HSTS. API is not publicly exposed — nginx reverse proxy on the dashboard container handles all external traffic. Remaining risks: (1) no privacy notice or consent mechanism published, (2) cross-border transfer safeguards not in place, (3) DPAs with Clerk/Stripe not signed. All financial calculations are deterministic and no automated decisions are made about users, which reduces AI-specific risk.
outcome.signOffNotes: Auth and encryption implemented. Requires privacy notice, consent mechanism, and DPAs before sign-off.

privacy.description: Collection of financial data (portfolio, SS benefits, income) and household composition

nonDiscrimination.description: Cost projections could vary by location in ways that correlate with demographics

dataProtection.description: Personal and financial data stored in cloud database

freedom.description: No impact on freedom of expression or movement

dignity.description: No profiling or scoring of individuals

consumerProtection.description: Users may rely on projections for major financial decisions

fairTreatment.description: No automated decisions — users make their own choices

financialHarm.measures: Prominent financial disclaimer ('not financial advice'), multiple scenario comparison to discourage over-reliance on single projection
financialHarm.monitoring: User feedback, support tickets mentioning financial decisions

privacyHarm.measures: Field-level encryption (planned), auth middleware (planned), DPAs with third parties (planned), GDPR privacy notice (planned)
privacyHarm.monitoring: Security audit skill, dependency audit, breach detection

discriminationHarm.measures: Bias testing across location categories and income levels, diverse location dataset (19 locations across 5 countries)
discriminationHarm.monitoring: Periodic bias testing via bias-testing skill

autonomyHarm.measures: No automated decisions — all projections presented as information, user controls all inputs

safetyHarm.measures: N/A — platform does not affect physical safety

reputationalHarm.measures: Data accuracy validation (validate-location-data skill), human review of AI-researched data, source citations

summary.notes: Primary risks are privacy/data protection (financial data handling) and consumer protection (reliance on projections). Discrimination risk is low because no decisions are made about users. Overall risk is medium — reducible to low once encryption, auth, and privacy notice are implemented.

humanOversight.modelJustification: The system does not make automated decisions about users. AI agents operate in a backend data research capacity with human review before data is published. Users interact only with deterministic calculations. Human-on-the-loop is appropriate because: (1) AI output (location cost data) is reviewed by admin before publication, (2) users make their own retirement decisions based on projections, (3) no real-time AI interaction requires human-in-the-loop intervention.

adminReview.description: All AI-researched location data reviewed by admin before database insertion via packages/tools/ pipeline

userControl.description: Users can override any cost estimate, create custom locations, and adjust all projection parameters
userControl.responseTime: Immediate — all controls available in dashboard

dataValidation.description: validate-location-data skill checks for anomalies, missing fields, and unreasonable values before data is seeded

escalation.description: No escalation path needed — no automated decisions affect users

killSwitch.description: AI agents can be disabled entirely; dashboard continues to function with existing static data
killSwitch.responseTime: Immediate — remove agent orchestration from tools pipeline

auditTrail.description: AdminLocationHistory tracks all data changes with version, changedBy, and timestamp

personnel.qualifications: Project owner with domain expertise in retirement planning, financial calculations, and cost research methodology
personnel.training: Familiarity with AI agent outputs, data validation procedures, and compliance requirements
personnel.escalation: N/A — single owner/operator during pre-launch phase

automationBias.additionalMeasures: AI-researched data is cross-referenced with government statistics, expat forums, and cost-of-living databases. Validate-location-data skill flags outliers.
automationBias.testingApproach: Compare AI-generated cost estimates against Numbeo, Expatistan, and local government data for each location

complianceGaps: No user-facing confidence scores for AI-researched data
No automated disagreement highlighting between AI and manual data

income.notes: Test whether cost projections and Monte Carlo success rates are proportionally accurate across income levels

age.notes: Test whether SS benefit calculations and RMD projections are accurate across age ranges

householdSize.notes: Test whether cost scaling is proportional and fair across household configurations

locationRegion.notes: Test whether FX drift and inflation projections are calibrated fairly across regions; check for systematic over/under-estimation

race.notes: Not directly applicable — platform does not collect race data or make decisions based on demographics. Location inclusion assessments cover racial prejudice but are informational only.

gender.notes: Not directly applicable — platform uses gender-neutral household member model. SS calculations use individual PIA/FRA regardless of gender.

remediations.action: Implement bias test suite in test-shared-functions skill covering income, age, household, and region dimensions
remediations.notes: Must complete before Colorado AI Act deadline (June 30)

remediations.action: Validate cost data accuracy across all 19 locations against independent sources
Add financial disclaimer to dashboard header/footer
remediations.notes: Required for consumer protection in all jurisdictions

socialSecurityModeling.withdrawalMethod: Delete SS data from household member settings or delete account

householdDataCollection.withdrawalMethod: Delete household members from settings or delete account

lifecycle.reConsentTriggers: Changes to data processing purposes, new third-party integrations, changes to privacy notice
lifecycle.crossBorderApproach: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) for EU-US transfers; DPAs with all processors

auditScope.methodDescription: Pre-launch audit — no consent records exist yet. This audit establishes the baseline schema and requirements for consent record-keeping.

auditFindings.recommendation: Implement consent collection at registration and for optional data (SS, household) before launch

auditFindings.finding: No consent record storage table in database schema
auditFindings.recommendation: Add ConsentRecord model to Prisma schema: userId, purpose, consentGiven, timestamp, privacyNoticeVersion, withdrawalDate
Draft and publish privacy notice before any user data collection begins

gaps: No withdrawal mechanism beyond account deletion

unauthorizedAccess.description: Clerk JWT auth middleware on all protected routes, per-tier rate limiting (free=60, basic=120, premium=300, admin=600 req/min) with Redis backing, nginx reverse proxy keeps API internal
unauthorizedAccess.evidence: packages/api/src/middleware/auth.ts, packages/api/src/middleware/rate-limit.ts, packages/dashboard/nginx.conf location /api/ block, docker-compose.prod.yml expose:3000 (not ports)

dataBreachExfiltration.description: AES-256-GCM field encryption for financial data at rest, TLS in transit via Tailscale certs with HSTS enforcement, nginx security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.)

sqlInjection.description: Prisma ORM with parameterized queries — no raw SQL anywhere in codebase
sqlInjection.evidence: All database access through prisma client methods

xss.description: React textContent rendering, no innerHTML with user data, Helmet CSP headers

csrf.description: JWT Bearer tokens via Clerk (no cookies for auth), same-origin API routing via nginx reverse proxy eliminates cross-origin attack surface
csrf.evidence: packages/api/src/middleware/auth.ts — Bearer token extraction, packages/dashboard/nginx.conf — same-origin /api/ proxy

bruteForce.description: Per-tier rate limiting (free=60, basic=120, premium=300, admin=600 req/min) with Redis backing and in-memory fallback

supplyChain.description: npm audit for vulnerability detection, package-lock.json committed

ddos.description: Per-tier rate limiting with Redis backing, nginx as front door with connection limits, Tailscale Funnel provides infrastructure-level protection

misconfiguration.description: nginx security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Permissions-Policy, server_tokens off), .env for secrets, .gitignore excludes certs/keys/credentials, Docker entrypoint auto-detects TLS config

waf.details: Planned for cloud deployment (Cloudflare or AWS WAF). nginx provides basic request filtering and security headers.

encryption_at_rest.details: AES-256-GCM application-layer encryption for financial fields (portfolio, income, SS PIAs). Key managed via ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY env var.

encryption_in_transit.details: Tailscale TLS certs (Let's Encrypt) with nginx termination on port 8443. HSTS enforced (max-age=31536000, includeSubDomains). HTTP→HTTPS redirect on port 8080. Tailscale Funnel provides auto TLS for public access. API container not publicly exposed — nginx reverse proxy on internal Docker network.

logging.details: Fastify built-in logger with Sentry error monitoring (opt-in via SENTRY_DSN env var). Dashboard has Sentry integration (opt-in via VITE_SENTRY_DSN).

monitoring.details: Sentry error tracking for API and dashboard. Health endpoint (/api/health) reports DB, Redis, encryption, and auth status. Offline/reconnect detection in dashboard.

backup.details: Planned: Daily automated PostgreSQL backups. Currently relies on Docker volume persistence.

accessControl.details: Separate database user (retirement), no root access, env-based secrets. Clerk JWT auth on all protected routes. Admin role enforcement on /api/admin/*. Per-tier rate limiting. nginx reverse proxy — API only accessible via internal Docker network.

networkSegmentation.details: Docker Compose internal network: API container exposed only via Docker 'expose' (port 3000, not bound to host). Dashboard nginx proxies /api/* to API internally. PostgreSQL and Redis are internal-only services with no host port binding. Only dashboard ports 8080/8443 are publicly accessible.

decisions.name: Retirement Cost Projection
decisions.description: Calculates projected annual costs for each retirement location over 35 years using per-category inflation rates and FX drift. All inputs are user-provided or from admin-managed location data. Output is informational — no action is taken automatically.
decisions.consequenceDescription: Users may rely on these projections to choose a retirement location or make financial plans. Inaccurate projections could lead to poor financial decisions. Mitigated by: financial disclaimer, multiple scenario comparison, user-adjustable parameters.

decisions.name: Monte Carlo Portfolio Simulation
decisions.description: Runs 5000 stochastic simulations of portfolio survival using user's portfolio balance, withdrawal rate, and market return assumptions. Returns success probability and percentile outcomes. No automated action — results are displayed for user interpretation.
decisions.estimatedVolume: Per user request (on-demand in Monte Carlo tab)
decisions.consequenceDescription: Users may use success probability to decide portfolio allocation or retirement timing. Simulations use simplified market models and may not capture tail risks. Mitigated by: range of scenarios, user-adjustable parameters, financial disclaimer.

decisions.name: Social Security Benefit Calculation
decisions.description: Calculates estimated SS benefits based on PIA, FRA, and claim age using SSA formulas. Includes spousal benefit, COLA, and trust fund depletion modeling. Deterministic calculation — no AI involved.
decisions.estimatedVolume: All users with SS data entered
decisions.consequenceDescription: Users may rely on these estimates to plan SS claiming strategy. Incorrect calculations could lead to suboptimal claiming decisions. Mitigated by: cross-check against SSA.gov calculator, audit-financial-calcs skill, disclaimer.

decisions.name: Subscription Tier Feature Gating
decisions.description: Restricts access to certain features based on subscription tier (free/basic/premium). This is a business decision, not an AI-driven decision.
decisions.consequenceDescription: Free tier users have limited features. This is standard SaaS gating and does not involve algorithmic decision-making.

legalEffects.justification: No legal effects — platform provides informational projections only

financialConsequences.justification: Users may make financial decisions based on projections, but no direct financial action is taken by the system

accessToServices.justification: Subscription gating is standard SaaS model, not algorithmic discrimination

humanReview.appealProcess: Users can contact support to dispute any data or projection. All inputs are user-modifiable.
humanReview.humanOverrideDescription: Users can override any cost estimate, adjust all calculation parameters, and create custom scenarios. Admin can update location data.
humanReview.escalationCriteria: User reports inaccurate calculation or data error
humanReview.reviewLogRetention: AdminLocationHistory retains all data changes indefinitely

formulaDisplay.implementation: Tax brackets, SS reduction formulas, and RMD divisors are documented in shared library source code

inputOutputTrace.implementation: Dashboard shows all inputs and derived outputs; users can adjust any parameter and see results change

sourceCitation.implementation: Location data includes source links; inclusion assessments cite official sources

explainability.shortExplanationTemplate: Your projected monthly cost in {location} is {amount}, based on {categories} with {inflation}% annual inflation over {years} years.
explainability.detailedExplanationTemplate: Detailed cost breakdown by category with per-category inflation rates, FX drift assumptions, and data sources available in Projections tab.
explainability.technicalExplanationTemplate: Calculation source code available in packages/shared/src/ — pure JavaScript functions with no external dependencies.
explainability.explanationAccessibility: Dashboard accessible via web browser; responsive design for mobile

purpose.systemPurpose: Help consumers compare retirement locations, project costs, simulate portfolio survival, and plan Social Security claiming strategies
purpose.useCases: 1. Compare monthly costs across 19 retirement locations. 2. Project costs over 35 years with inflation and FX drift. 3. Run Monte Carlo simulations for portfolio survival. 4. Model Social Security benefits with spousal and delay strategies. 5. Explore neighborhoods, services, and inclusion assessments per location.
purpose.deployEnv: Cloud SaaS — Vercel/Cloudflare (dashboard) + Railway/Render (API) + managed PostgreSQL

architecture.foundationModel: Claude (Opus/Sonnet) by Anthropic — used only for backend data research, not in user-facing calculations
architecture.fineTuning: None — Claude is used via API with prompt engineering only
architecture.inputTypes: User: financial data, household composition, location preferences. AI agent: public cost data sources, government statistics
architecture.outputTypes: Dashboard: financial projections, charts, comparison tables, Monte Carlo results. AI agent: structured location cost data (JSON)
architecture.pipeline: 1. AI agents research cost data from public sources. 2. Admin reviews and approves data. 3. Data seeded to PostgreSQL. 4. Dashboard reads data via API. 5. Client-side pure functions compute projections. 6. Results displayed to user.
architecture.keyFactors: Per-category inflation rates, FX drift assumptions, SS benefit formulas, IRS tax brackets, Monte Carlo market return parameters
architecture.confidenceMethod: Monte Carlo provides probability distributions (p5/p25/median/p75/p95); no single-point predictions
architecture.fallback: Dashboard functions with static JSON data if API or AI agents are unavailable

dataAccuracy.description: Cost data is AI-researched and may not reflect current prices in all locations
dataAccuracy.mitigation: Human review of all data, validate-location-data skill, source citations

modelBias.description: English-language source bias may underrepresent local pricing in Latin American locations
modelBias.mitigation: Cross-reference with local government data, expat community sources

temporalValidity.description: Cost data requires periodic updates; exchange rates and inflation assumptions may become stale
temporalValidity.mitigation: Periodic data refresh via AI agents, user-adjustable parameters

geographicLimitation.description: Limited to 19 locations in 5 countries — does not cover all possible retirement destinations
geographicLimitation.mitigation: Users can add custom locations with their own cost estimates

financialAdvice.description: Platform provides informational projections, not financial advice
financialAdvice.mitigation: Prominent disclaimer, encourage consulting financial professionals

monteCarloSimplification.description: Monte Carlo uses simplified market models (normal distribution returns, constant inflation assumptions)
monteCarloSimplification.mitigation: Display as probability ranges, not guarantees; user can adjust parameters

oversight.model: Human-on-the-Loop — admin reviews AI data before publication, users control all inputs
oversight.overrideCapability: Users override any cost, parameter, or assumption. Admin overrides location data.
oversight.qualifications: Domain expertise in retirement planning and financial calculations
oversight.monitorFrequency: Daily during active development; weekly post-launch

aiSystemDisclosure.copyApproach: Disclose that location cost data is researched with AI assistance and reviewed by humans
aiSystemDisclosure.complianceNotes: EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency; Colorado deployer disclosure; California AI Transparency Act

financialDisclaimer.copyApproach: Prominent disclaimer: 'This tool provides informational projections for retirement planning purposes only. It is not financial, tax, or legal advice. Consult a qualified professional before making financial decisions.'
financialDisclaimer.complianceNotes: Required in all jurisdictions for financial sector consumer-facing applications

dataCollectionNotice.copyApproach: At-registration notice listing data categories collected, purposes, retention, and rights
dataCollectionNotice.complianceNotes: GDPR Art. 13/14, CCPA notice at collection, LGPD, LFPDPPP

cookieConsent.copyApproach: Cookie banner for localStorage usage disclosure; strictly necessary cookies exempt
cookieConsent.complianceNotes: ePrivacy Directive; localStorage may be treated as cookies under EU law

aiContentLabeling.copyApproach: Label AI-researched cost data with indicator (e.g., 'Data sourced with AI assistance, human-reviewed')
aiContentLabeling.complianceNotes: EU AI Act Art. 50 content labeling; California SB 942

algorithmicDecisionDisclosure.copyApproach: Disclose that Monte Carlo simulations and cost projections use algorithmic calculations; provide methodology documentation
algorithmicDecisionDisclosure.complianceNotes: Colorado AI Act deployer disclosure; NYC Local Law 144 (if employment-adjacent)

copyDesign.testingApproach: User testing with 5-10 retirement-age adults before launch

dataSources.name: Anthropic Claude API
dataSources.description: Foundation model used via API for location cost data research. No fine-tuning or custom training data.

dataSources.name: Public cost-of-living databases
dataSources.description: AI agents reference Numbeo, Expatistan, government statistics, and expat community sources to research location costs
dataSources.acquisition: Public web sources, cited per data point

dataSources.name: IRS/SSA official data
dataSources.description: Tax brackets, SS benefit formulas, RMD tables hardcoded from official government publications

characteristics.totalVolume: 19 locations with ~50 data fields each; IRS/SSA formula parameters
characteristics.languages: English (primary), with cost data from French, Spanish, Portuguese sources
characteristics.geographicCoverage: US (9 locations), France (4), Spain (1), Portugal (1), Panama (2), Ireland (1)
characteristics.temporalRange: 2024-2026 cost data; IRS 2026 tax brackets; SSA current formulas

copyrightAudit.copyrightStatus: No copyright concerns — AI used via commercial API, not trained on user data

optOut.complianceApproach: Platform does not train AI models. Claude is accessed via commercial API. Location data is compiled from public sources with citations.

gpaiSummary.policyMeasures: Anthropic (Claude provider) is responsible for GPAI compliance under EU AI Act. Platform is a deployer, not a provider, of the foundation model.

EU.disclosureApproach: Disclose use of Claude API for data research; note Anthropic's GPAI compliance; provide data source citations

US-CA.disclosureApproach: AB 2013 training data transparency — disclose that AI assists in data research; platform does not train models

BR.disclosureApproach: LGPD data processing disclosure includes AI-assisted data collection methodology

roles.authority: Full authority over system design, data, deployment, and compliance decisions
Privacy notice, consent mechanisms, DSAR responses, DPA negotiations
Architecture decisions, security implementation, code review

meetings.name: Data Quality Review
meetings.attendees: Project Owner — validate location data accuracy

critical.definition: Data breach affecting financial data (portfolio, SS benefits), unauthorized access to user accounts, complete service outage

high.definition: Partial data exposure, authentication bypass, significant calculation errors affecting financial projections

medium.definition: Minor data quality issues, non-critical feature outage, rate limiting failures

low.definition: UI bugs, cosmetic issues, minor data inaccuracies in non-financial fields

detection.actions: Triage severity, assign incident lead, begin investigation

containment.actions: Isolate affected systems, revoke compromised credentials, disable affected features if needed

investigation.actions: Root cause analysis, scope of impact assessment, affected user identification

notification.timeline: 72 hours for GDPR (EU), without unreasonable delay for CCPA (CA)
notification.actions: Notify affected users, notify relevant DPA (GDPR), notify AG (CCPA if >500 CA residents)

remediation.actions: Fix root cause, deploy patch, verify fix, update security controls

postMortem.actions: Document lessons learned, update incident playbook, implement preventive measures

EU.whom: Lead supervisory authority (based on main establishment)

retention.logStorage: Incident logs retained for 7 years (compliance requirement)
retention.accessControl: Incident logs accessible only to Project Owner and designated compliance personnel

regulators.method: Official notification form per DPA requirements

public.when: Only if required by law or >1000 users affected

automatedDecisions.method: No automated decisions — users make all decisions. Financial calculations are tools, not decisions.

inApp.description: Planned: Settings page with data export, deletion, and consent management

legalRetention.howInvoked: Financial records may need 7-year retention for tax compliance
legalRetention.appealProcess: User notified of retention reason and remaining duration

locationCostData.labelText: Cost data researched with AI assistance, human-reviewed

locationCostData.visibility: Visible on hover/click of data source indicator

neighborhoodDescriptions.labelText: AI-assisted research, verified against public sources

inclusionAssessments.labelText: Assessment compiled from official sources with AI research assistance

euMetadata.easyAccessMechanism: Planned: 'About this data' link on each location page explaining AI research methodology and data sources

systemInfo.description: Multi-user commercial SaaS for retirement cost comparison, financial projections, and Monte Carlo portfolio simulation with AI-assisted data research

gaps: DPAs with third-party processors not signed (Clerk, Stripe)
Financial disclaimer not displayed in dashboard
outcome.remediationPlan: Auth, encryption, tier enforcement, input sanitization, and OWASP hardening implemented. Remaining: privacy notice + consent + disclaimers (Phase 1), then bias testing + DPAs (Phase 2), then security documentation (Phase 3). All before target launch (2026-09-01).

systemInfo.systemDescription: Multi-user SaaS using AI agents for data research; deterministic calculations for user-facing projections
systemInfo.jurisdiction: EU, US (CA, CO, TX, NYC, IL, UT), Brazil, Mexico, Peru

admin.description: SaaS administrators managing location data and reviewing AI agent outputs

consumers.description: End users of the retirement planning dashboard

aiBasics.topic: Understanding AI in the retirement planning platform
aiBasics.contentSummary: How AI agents research location data, what AI does vs. does not do in the platform, limitations of AI-researched data

dataReview.topic: Reviewing and approving AI-generated location data
dataReview.contentSummary: Using validate-location-data skill, comparing AI output to reference sources, flagging anomalies

complianceAwareness.topic: AI compliance requirements for the platform
complianceAwareness.contentSummary: Overview of EU AI Act, Colorado AI Act, and Brazil PL 2338 requirements relevant to this platform

euArt4.personDefinition: Any person involved in the operation, deployment, or oversight of the AI system — includes admin users who review AI agent output and make publication decisions
euArt4.implementationApproach: Self-paced documentation covering AI capabilities, limitations, and compliance obligations. Admin must complete before reviewing AI-generated data.

selectedModels.usage: Agent orchestration for complex data research and refinement tasks
Standard data research, cost updates, and location data validation

claudeOpus.environment: Development/tools only — not in production runtime
claudeOpus.dataProcessed: Public cost data, location research queries

claudeSonnet.environment: Development/tools only — not in production runtime
claudeSonnet.dataProcessed: Public cost data, location research queries

llmSelector.notes: LLMs are used exclusively in the development/tools pipeline for researching and updating location cost data. No LLM is called at runtime when users interact with the dashboard. All user-facing calculations are deterministic pure JavaScript functions.
